name: Accent sweep
description: This algorithm recreates a very specific aspect of the Roland TB-303's circuitry. There is a lengthy write-up on it on the Devil Fish page here, but essentially it creates an AD (attack/decay) envelope which will reach a higher maximum level if repeatedly triggered.
gui_description: The display shows the input (dark) and output (light) signals.

parameters.name: Input mode
parameters.description: Sets how the input is treated: Direct feeds the input signal directly into the AD shaper. Trigger uses the input to trigger an internal pulse, which is fed into the shaper.

parameters.name: Trigger length
parameters.description: If the input mode is Trigger, the length of the pulse.

parameters.name: Trigger voltage
parameters.description: If the input mode is Trigger, the voltage of the pulse.

parameters.name: Shape
parameters.description: This is the position of the pot (potentiometer), if you studied the Devil Fish write-up. At 0% the output is quite close to the input; at 100% it is heavily affected by the AD shaper.

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: Applies a gain to the output shape.

parameters.name: Charge
parameters.description: The value of the charge resistor.

parameters.name: Discharge
parameters.description: The value of the discharge resistor.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

input_ports.name: Input
input_ports.description: The bus to use as input.

output_ports.name: Output
output_ports.description: Processed output signal from the algorithm

output_ports.name: Output
output_ports.description: The bus to use as output.
